supply_chain.dependencies: Polymer resins and films, paper, inks, solvents, adhesives, aluminum, and chemicals constitute the major raw materials. Not significantly dependent on any one supplier.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to geopolitical conflicts, natural disasters, health crises, wars, acts of terrorism, shortages in manufacturing inputs, loss of key suppliers, freight cost fluctuations, limitations on shipping capacity, severe weather events and climate change impacts on agricultural productivity

regulatory_risks: Environmental regulations related to greenhouse gas emissions, biodiversity and deforestation
Human rights due diligence regulations
Extended producer responsibility (EPR) regulations
PFAS restrictions in packaging materials in various U.S. states
Plastic bans or mandates to reduce plastic use
Mandates to use post-consumer recycled (PCR) content
Data privacy laws and regulations (U.S., Europe, China)
Modern slavery reporting requirements
Climate-related disclosure rules
ESG government regulations
OECD and G20 BEPS Pillar Two rule (15% global minimum tax)
Economic sanctions administered by U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control, U.S. Department of State, and U.S. Department of Commerce
Export control regulations
Anti-corruption and anti-bribery laws
Competition and antitrust regulations
Healthcare packaging regulations (FDA and international standards)
Food and beverage packaging regulations
Environmental laws relating to air, soil, and water quality, handling, discharge, storage, and disposal of substances
Workplace health and safety regulations
Changes in tax laws or fiscal regimes in multiple jurisdictions
Trade restrictions, sanctions, quotas, and tariffs
Retroactive duties on aluminum imports from China

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions against Russia due to Ukraine conflict
U.S., European Union, and UK sanctions compliance
Sales to entities in countries subject to economic sanctions
Sanctions that could impact operations in certain countries
